name: app.R
DEPLOY.md
content:
# DAM Monitor Combiner — Shinylive Export & Deploy Guide

## What you need (one-time setup on YOUR machine)
```r
install.packages("shinylive")   # if not already installed
```

---

## Step 1 — Export the app to a static folder

Open R or RStudio and run:

```r
shinylive::export(
  appdir = "path/to/shinylive_app",   # folder containing app.R
  destdir = "path/to/shinylive_app/site"
)
```

This creates a `site/` folder containing plain HTML/JS/CSS — no R server needed.

---

## Step 2 — Deploy (pick one option)

### Option A: GitHub Pages (recommended — free, permanent URL)

1. Create a new GitHub repository (e.g. `dam-monitor-combiner`)
2. Push the contents of `site/` to the `main` branch
3. Go to repo Settings → Pages → Source: Deploy from branch → `main` → `/root`
4. Your app is live at: `https://YOUR_USERNAME.github.io/dam-monitor-combiner/`
5. Share that URL with your coworkers — done

### Option B: Share the folder directly (no internet needed)

1. Put the `site/` folder on a shared network drive your coworkers can access
2. They open `site/index.html` directly in their browser

> ⚠️  Note: Chrome blocks local file access by default.
> Workaround: open Chrome with `--allow-file-access-from-files` flag,
> or use Firefox which allows it by default.
> GitHub Pages (Option A) avoids this issue entirely.

### Option C: Python one-liner local server (temporary sharing)

If you just want to demo it quickly from your machine:
```bash
cd path/to/site
python3 -m http.server 8080
# coworkers on same network open: http://YOUR_IP:8080
```

---

## Coworker experience

- Open the URL in any browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
- No installation, no login, no accounts
- Upload Monitor 1, 2, 3 → Run → Download combined file
- Files never leave their browser (processed entirely in WebAssembly)

---

## Updating the app

Edit `app.R`, re-run `shinylive::export()`, re-push to GitHub Pages.
GitHub Pages updates within ~1 minute.

---

## Troubleshooting

- **`data.table` not loading in WebAssembly**: if you see a package error in the browser,
  run `shinylive::check_packages("data.table")` first. If unsupported, the app.R
  has a fallback using base R `read.table` / `write.table` instead.
- **Blank page**: make sure you are serving via HTTP (Option A, B with Firefox, or Option C),
  not opening index.html directly in Chrome.
